scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings only for the listed files and tags each with introduced_in_diff:true|false per AC-2.6 default-safe rule. Tagging applies only to in-scope (.md) findings; skipped out-of-scope files carry introduced_in_diff as provenance metadata, not finding tags.
reason: No changed files matched prompt-inspector scope (~/.claude/commands/*.md, ~/.claude/agents/*.md). All 3 changed files in this /dev cycle are Python (.py).
scope_mismatch_detail: changed_files contained only .py files, while prompt-inspector scope is limited to ~/.claude/commands/*.md and ~/.claude/agents/*.md. No in-scope Markdown files were available for inspection. Python files, including docstrings and comments, are out of scope for prompt-inspector. They must not generate prompt-inspector findings — separate code-cleanliness inspectors handle that. Avoiding silent expansion prevents overlapping responsibility and false-positive findings on legitimate Python documentation.

inspector_scope.principle: rules not stories — applied to Markdown command/agent documentation only

domain_check.rationale: All three cycle-diff files are Python source (hook implementation + test code), not command/agent Markdown documentation. Prompt-inspector audits .md files for verbose explanatory sections (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, etc.); Python docstrings and comments are outside the declared role scope. Per orchestrator instruction: when no .md files appear in --changed-files, return findings=[] with an out-of-domain note rather than synthesizing out-of-scope findings.

skipped_out_of_scope_files.reason: Python file — prompt-inspector targets .md command/agent documentation only
Python file — prompt-inspector targets .md command/agent documentation only
Python file — prompt-inspector targets .md command/agent documentation only

summary.note: No Markdown command/agent documentation files in this cycle's diff. No verbosity inspection performed. Default-safe rule: absence of findings is not interpreted as cleanliness assertion over .py files. Status set to 'not_applicable' (rather than 'pass') to prevent close.md or downstream consumers from interpreting empty findings as a cycle-wide cleanliness pass.

new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.name: Mechanism (ii) — inspector-side tagging
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.description: In --changed-files mode, prompt-inspector emits findings for the listed files and tags each with an explicit introduced_in_diff:bool field. close.md uses this tag per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule.
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.applied_in_this_report_detail: Inspector-side tagging with introduced_in_diff applies only to findings from in-scope Markdown files. No tags were emitted on findings because no findings were produced (all 3 changed files are out-of-scope .py). The introduced_in_diff:true field on skipped_out_of_scope_files entries is provenance metadata documenting that those files originated in this diff, not a finding tag.
new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.default_safe_behavior: Per agent contract: absent/null/untagged file-level finding output is treated as pre-existing/advisory/non-blocking by close.md. Empty findings means close.md has nothing from prompt-inspector to block on for this diff.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Re-consulted codex (gpt-5.5 xhigh, session 019e4492-6af9-7742-abaa-9d110f5bc1ab) on the empty-findings + scope-mismatch conclusion. Codex confirmed the draft on all 3 challenge questions and returned 5 concrete PROPOSED_FIXes (all applied): (1) keep findings=[] with explicit scope_mismatch wording; (2) explicit declaration that Python files/docstrings/comments are out of scope; (3) report status as 'not_applicable' rather than plain 'pass' to prevent misreading 0-violations as cycle-wide cleanliness; (4) --changed-files mode should auto-skip out-of-scope extensions via top-level skipped_out_of_scope_files metadata, not synthetic findings; (5) introduced_in_diff tagging applies only to in-scope findings, not skipped metadata. Codex agreed: do NOT silently expand inspector to Python verbosity — would create overlapping responsibility with code/style inspectors.

findings.issue: Confirm empty findings + scope_mismatch is correct conclusion
findings.proposed_fix: Keep findings: [] and add wording: scope_mismatch: true; changed_files contained only .py files, while prompt-inspector scope is limited to .md command/agent docs. No in-scope Markdown files were available for inspection.
findings.rationale: Applied in scope_mismatch + scope_mismatch_detail top-level fields.

findings.issue: Do not silently produce Python verbosity findings — would expand inspector beyond declared .md scope and create overlapping responsibility with code/style inspectors
findings.proposed_fix: Add explicit wording: Python files, including docstrings and comments, are out of scope for prompt-inspector. They must not generate prompt-inspector findings.

findings.issue: False-negative risk exists only if consumers misread '0 violations' as 'whole /dev cycle has no verbosity/code-quality issues'
findings.proposed_fix: Report status as not_applicable or no_in_scope_files rather than plain pass: result: 'not_applicable'; reason: 'No changed files matched prompt-inspector scope.'
findings.rationale: Applied as top-level result: 'not_applicable' + reason field, and summary.status: 'not_applicable'.

findings.issue: --changed-files mode should auto-skip out-of-scope extensions cleanly, not emit one finding per Python file. Out-of-scope files are metadata, not violations.
findings.proposed_fix: Include skipped files in a top-level field: skipped_out_of_scope_files: [...], while keeping findings: [].
findings.rationale: Applied as top-level skipped_out_of_scope_files array with per-file metadata (path, extension, reason, introduced_in_diff).

findings.issue: Mechanism documentation is appropriate, but only for in-scope findings. Since there are no findings, introduced_in_diff should not appear as synthetic findings.
findings.proposed_fix: Add wording: changed-files mechanism: inspector-side tagging with introduced_in_diff applies only to findings from in-scope Markdown files; no tags were emitted because no findings were produced.
findings.rationale: Applied in new_vs_preexisting_mechanism.applied_in_this_report_detail clarifying that introduced_in_diff:true on skipped_out_of_scope_files entries is provenance metadata, not finding tags.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex PROPOSED_FIX entries applied. Top-level structure: result=not_applicable, scope_mismatch=true with detailed wording explicitly excluding Python from scope, skipped_out_of_scope_files array for clean metadata, empty findings: [], mechanism note clarifying tagging applies only to in-scope findings. No findings invented from Python files; scope held strictly to .md mandate. Compatible with prior pass's findings=[] conclusion while strengthening structure per codex guidance.
